etymology.description: 醫(醫) (yī, "medical") + 藥(藥) (yào, "medicine") + 費(費) (fèi, "expense; fee; cost"). The combination expresses the concept of costs associated with medical treatment.
etymology.interpretation: 醫(醫) (yī, "medical") denotes aspects related to healing and medicine, implying the care of health by professionals. Together with 藥(藥) (yào, "medicine"), it specifies the physical medications and treatments used. Adding 費(費) (fèi, "expense") highlights the monetary aspect, resulting in the coordinated expense for medical purposes. Thus, the term directly refers to costs incurred for medical treatments and pharmaceuticals.

examples.chinese: 保险公司会报销部分医药费(保險公司會報銷部分醫藥費)。
examples.pinyin: Bǎoxiǎn gōngsī huì bàoxiāo bùfèn yīyàofèi.
examples.english: The insurance company will reimburse part of the medical expenses.

examples.chinese: 他的医药费(他的醫藥費)非常高，需要家人帮助(需要家人幫助)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yīyàofèi fēicháng gāo, xūyào jiārén bāngzhù.
examples.english: His medical expenses are very high and require family support.

examples.chinese: 政府提供补助以减轻居民的医药费(政府提供補助以減輕居民的醫藥費)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèngfǔ tígōng bǔzhù yǐ jiǎnqīng jūmín de yīyàofèi.
examples.english: The government provides subsidies to alleviate residents' medical expenses.
